spec_paraphrase: Super experts carry outsized influence on model output; detection signal is max(|down_proj_output|).
paper_verbatim: SEs are characterized by rare but extreme activation outliers in the output of the down_proj
notes: Spec adopts the SE concept and the down_proj-output magnitude signal directly.

spec_paraphrase: Pruning SEs causes catastrophic quality collapse (e.g., -21.7% on Qwen3-30B).
paper_verbatim: prune just three out of 6,144 causes Qwen3-30B-A3B to generate repetitive and uninformative outputs
notes: Spec cites the -21.7% drop verbatim as motivation for SE protection.

paper_verbatim: Hl = MoE(LNmoe(Hl')) + Hl'
notes: Standard pre-LN MoE decoder equation; background, not load-bearing for Stage 1 spec. No finding.

paper_verbatim: Hl' = O^l + H^{l-1}, O^l = MHSA(LNmhsa(H^{l-1}))
notes: Pre-LN MHSA preliminary; background, not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: G = softmax(Hl' WG)
notes: Standard router preliminary; background.

paper_verbatim: sum_{i in Top-k(Gj)} Gji * FFN(LNmoe(Hl'_j))
notes: Top-k aggregation preliminary; background.

spec_paraphrase: Spec assumes gate_proj/up_proj/down_proj structure throughout (Stage 1 hooks down_proj output; Stage 3 covariances for each).
paper_verbatim: FFN(X) = (sigma(X Wg) elementwise* X Wu) Wd
notes: SwiGLU expert structure is the architecture spec operates on.

spec_paraphrase: Phase A collects max(|down_proj_output|) for SE detection — same signal.
paper_verbatim: a small subset of experts consistently produces extreme activation outliers in the output of their down_proj layers
notes: Spec uses exactly the down_proj-output magnitude signal.

paper_verbatim: when all SEs are pruned, MAs are completely eliminated
notes: Causal ablation evidence; spec uses the SE-protection conclusion but not the experiment itself.

spec_paraphrase: Per-layer z-score: blacklisted(l,e) = max_activation(l,e) > μ_l + 2.5·σ_l. No global P99.5, no 0.1·a_max floor, no l∈L pre-filter.
paper_verbatim: a_{l,e} > P99.5 and a_{l,e} > (1/10) * a_max and l in L
notes: Major deviation from canonical SE detection criterion. Justification is paper-internal/algorithmic (avoids two-pass global stats and MA-formation layer detection); sanctioned as ch12. Note: paper Table 9 (Appendix I) shows SE counts robust across (P95..P99.9, 0.07..0.10·amax), but the per-layer z-score is a different statistic entirely — not within the paper's robustness band.

paper_verbatim: No specific dataset is designated for identifying SEs
notes: Spec uses 256 calibration samples (line 121); data-agnosticism implicit. No finding.
Empirical reference values; not ingested by spec. Could serve as ground truth for compliance test (informational).
DeepSeek-R1 ground truth; spec targets Qwen3-30B-A3B only. Background.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat 1782 2 0.11% ...
notes: DSV2-Lite ground truth; out of scope for spec. Background.

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 256 1 0.39% ...
notes: Mixtral ground truth; out of scope for spec.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B  Layer 1 Expert 68, Layer 2 Expert 92, Layer 3 Expert 82
notes: This is the canonical SE ground truth for the spec's target model. Spec does not name these explicitly; with D1's per-layer z-score, output may differ. Reproducibility check should compare detector output to this set. Logged as info — spec omission is not a violation, but a regression test would help.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat  Layer 3 Expert 54, Layer 4 Expert 38
notes: Out of scope (Qwen3 only).

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 Layer 1 Expert 3
notes: Out of scope.

paper_verbatim: SEs ... accounting for less than 0.5% of all experts
notes: Spec's 5% global cap is 10× the paper's <0.5% empirical bound — generous but loose. D2 frames this as a safety guardrail; treated as ch12-sanctioned. Per-layer cap of 4 is empirical/conservative. Drift in stated bound, not a violation.

spec_paraphrase: All 40 MoE layers profiled; no MA-formation pre-filtering.
paper_verbatim: some experts in the final layers also exhibit extreme activation outliers. However ... they do not hold the same level of significance as SEs.
notes: Without l∈L filter, late-layer outlier experts (Table 7: L47E48, L47E100 on Qwen3) could match per-layer z>2.5 and be added to the blacklist. The 4-per-layer cap and 5% global cap (D2) bound the damage, but this is a real false-positive risk. Sanctioned via D1's 'per-layer z-score is conservative in practice (z>10 typically)' — but Table 7 outlier experts may approach that threshold.

paper_verbatim: Drop Rate 21.68%
notes: Spec cites the headline number from Table 3.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite ... 27.17%
notes: Out-of-scope model.
Out-of-scope model.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 ... Drop 97.61%
notes: Out-of-scope model; reasoning collapse on DeepSeek-R1.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B (thinking) ... -93.62%
notes: Reasoning-mode collapse evidence; spec uses the conclusion (SEs critical), not this number.

paper_verbatim: the model consistently generated repetitive responses ... continuing until it reached the maximum output length
notes: Diagnostic behavior; could inform a regression test in Stage 6 evals but spec does not specify one.

spec_paraphrase: SE-protection rationale (Phase B exists because SEs are critical).
paper_verbatim: SEs constitute the primary source, MAs act as the intermediate bridge, and ASs manifest their effects within the attention mechanism.
notes: Causal chain underlies why Phase B exists in spec.

paper_verbatim: the router scores assigned to SEs for the first token ... are exceptionally large
notes: Alternate router-score-based detector mechanism; spec uses down_proj-output magnitude only. Not load-bearing.

paper_verbatim: Dsink = 1 - (1/H) * sum ...
notes: Diagnostic metric; not a Stage 1 detection ingredient.

paper_verbatim: after SE pruning, the Dsink remains consistently high, at approximately or even exceeding 90%
notes: Empirical Dsink value; out of scope.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B Original 8.70 ; Prune SEs ... 59.86
notes: Could be regression test but spec doesn't specify.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite Original 6.31 ...
notes: Out of scope.

spec_paraphrase: All 40 MoE layers profiled (no l∈L filter).
paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B Outlier Experts: Layer 1 Expert 8, Layer 47 Expert 48, Layer 47 Expert 100
notes: These are explicit non-SE outlier experts the paper warns against. Spec's omission of l∈L filter (D1) means a per-layer z-score detector at L47 could classify them as 'super experts' if their max activation is far above the L47 layer mean. This is a concrete false-positive case D1 does not explicitly address.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 Outlier Experts: ...
notes: Out-of-scope model.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B 0-47, Layer 1 SE 68 down_proj shape (2048,768), SW (940, 711); ...
notes: Spec protects the entire SE expert (no compression) so SW row-level granularity is not needed for Phase B. Could matter if spec ever quantizes SEs at row level — currently it does not.

paper_verbatim: mse_loss = (1/(N*D)) * sum ...
notes: SAE auxiliary objective; not load-bearing.

paper_verbatim: l1_loss = lambda * (1/N) * sum ...
notes: SAE sparsity penalty; auxiliary.

paper_verbatim: CCDF(x) = P(X > x)
notes: Statistical preliminary.

paper_verbatim: P(X > x) ∝ x^{-alpha}
notes: Heavy-tail justification; not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B alpha=1.50, x_min=74.00 ; ...
notes: Power-law fit values; not used by spec.

spec_paraphrase: Per-layer z-score (mean+2.5σ) is outside the paper's robustness band.
paper_verbatim: 3 SEs identified for every combination of percentile in {P95,P99,P99.5,P99.9} and amax-fraction in {0.07,0.08,0.09,0.10}
notes: The paper demonstrates robustness across global-percentile/amax-fraction combos. Spec's per-layer z-score is a different statistical family (per-layer distribution-shape dependent), so paper's robustness guarantee does NOT transfer. D1 acknowledges deviation; informational risk is that per-layer z-score may be miscalibrated for a layer with naturally heavy-tailed activations.

paper_verbatim: Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base / Qwen3-30B-A3B / Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507 / Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507: Layer 1 Expert 68, Layer 2 Expert 92, Layer 3 Expert 82
notes: SE invariance across post-training variants; could be reused for caching but spec re-detects.

spec_paraphrase: Phase B emits a blacklist set (functional equivalent to SE set S) keyed by (layer, expert).
paper_verbatim: Input: Model with E experts per layer, calibration dataset D ; Output: Set of Super Experts S
notes: I/O contract matches at the interface level.

spec_paraphrase: All 40 MoE layers profiled; no MA-pattern detection.
paper_verbatim: Stage 1: Calibration of MA-formation Layers. L <- empty; ... if MA pattern detected in Hl(x): L <- L union {l}
notes: Spec explicitly skips Algorithm 1 Stage 1 (MA-formation layer detection). D1 sanctions this on grounds of avoiding two-pass collection and conservative behavior of per-layer z-score. Same deviation as #eq6_se_def.

spec_paraphrase: max(|down_proj_output|) collected per (layer, expert) over all calibration tokens.
paper_verbatim: a_{l,e} <- max_{x in D} | h_{l,e}(x) * W^{l,e}_{down_proj} |
notes: Signal collection matches exactly. The paraphrase drift is minor — paper writes |h·W_d| (the down_proj output, post-multiplication); spec writes max(|down_proj_output|). These are equivalent; no real deviation.

spec_paraphrase: Per-layer mean+2.5σ instead of global P99.5 AND 0.1·a_max.
paper_verbatim: if a_{l,e} > P99.5 and a_{l,e} > (1/10) a_max: S <- S union {(l,e)}
notes: Same core deviation as #eq6_se_def. Paper is emphatic that thresholds are GLOBAL across L; spec uses per-layer statistics. D1 sanctions.

paper_verbatim: Extreme activation outliers ... are propagated into the hidden states between decoders via residual summation, progressively leading to massive activation.
notes: Mechanism background; spec uses the conclusion (protect SEs).

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses 256 calibration samples (single forward pass over all 40 layers).
paper_verbatim: MAs are computed using 100 input samples from the C4 dataset, each with a length of 2K.
notes: Spec exceeds paper's 100-sample minimum (256 > 100); both well above the 'reasonable size' bound.

paper_verbatim: MoE models exhibit the progressive formation of systematic outliers by SEs across multiple layers
notes: Justifies multi-layer profiling; spec already iterates all layers.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-V2-Lite ... first layer utilizes dense MLPs
notes: Out-of-scope architecture quirk (Qwen3-30B-A3B is uniformly MoE post-first-decoder).

paper_verbatim: the distribution of SEs remains highly stable, regardless of variations in the input data domain.
notes: Justifies free choice of calibration corpus; spec implicitly relies on this.

paper_verbatim: up to 100,000 times larger
notes: Magnitude background; spec's z>2.5 threshold corresponds to much smaller separation but the data clusters this strongly anyway.

paper_verbatim: the AS completely disappears.
notes: Visual diagnostic; spec uses the conclusion.

paper_verbatim: maintaining ASs is essential
notes: Spec protects SEs (which preserves ASs) without naming AS-preservation directly.

paper_verbatim: This phenomenon typically occurs in a single layer ... or in just a few layers ... starting from the initial decoder layers
notes: Confirms paper's expectation that L is small/early; spec's omission of L pre-filter (D1) is sanctioned but creates the false-positive risk on late-layer outlier experts (Table 7).

paper_verbatim: random pruning ... globally ... or ... layer-controlled (LC) random pruning
notes: Evaluation methodology; not used by spec (spec is implementation, not eval). No finding.

paper_verbatim: random pruning has a negligible impact
notes: Evaluation result; out of spec scope.

paper_verbatim: prune just three out of 6,144
notes: Spec does not pin SE-count to 3 (D1's per-layer z-score may emit different cardinality). 4-per-layer × 40 layers = up to 160 candidates capped by 5% global ≈ ~50 candidates max. Substantially looser than paper's expected 3. Risk: over-blacklisting reduces compression ratio. D1+D2 sanction this; treated as ch12 risk.

paper_verbatim: DeepSeek-R1 ... SE Count 10
notes: Out-of-scope model.

paper_verbatim: less than 0.5% of all experts
notes: Same as #sec3.2.2_concl: spec's 5% cap is 10× the paper's empirical bound. D2 sanctions as safety guardrail.

spec_paraphrase: max(|down_proj_output|) hooked on the down_proj output.
paper_verbatim: we compute the maximum output magnitudes to the down_proj for all experts across all layers
notes: Spec uses exact paper signal.

paper_verbatim: https://github.com/ZunhaiSu/Super-Experts-Profilling
notes: Reference implementation pointer; not load-bearing.

paper_verbatim: this behavior is independent of the input dataset
notes: Justifies free corpus choice; not directly used.

paper_verbatim: removing the SWs consistently leads to repetitive and uninformative outputs
notes: SE-internal granularity; spec protects whole SE expert.

paper_verbatim: in dense LLMs, the single FFN layer ... can be seen as playing a role analogous to that of an SE.
notes: Generalization note; out of spec scope.

paper_verbatim: top features associated with the SWs neuron consistently exhibit pronounced activation at the end of text token
notes: Mechanistic interpretability note; not load-bearing.
Architectural dims; spec inherits from HF model config at runtime.

paper_verbatim: the estimated tail exponent α is below 2
notes: Statistical justification; not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: The sink token subsequently produces activation outliers ... ultimately emerge as attention sinks.
notes: End-to-end mechanism; spec uses conclusion (protect SEs).

paper_verbatim: the distribution of SEs remains highly consistent across these varied domains
notes: Cross-domain robustness; informs free corpus choice.

paper_verbatim: All models, datasets, experimental setups, and hyperparameters used in this work are thoroughly documented.
notes: Reproducibility statement; not load-bearing.
